_comment: 9 synthetic cases. generate.py renders the images deterministically. run.sh feeds each (image, question) to raw-Opus-via-API and to bmg-pro-MCP-with-intent via the profile noted on each case. README displays the outputs side-by-side, showing prose vs profile-structured JSON.

cases.question: Read the bar chart and give me the value for each quarter and which quarter is highest. Be specific about the numbers.
cases.intent: extract the exact numeric value of each bar, the chart title, and the axis label
cases.what_it_tests: Reading specific values off a chart — the canonical 'Opus says "a chart with bars going down"' failure mode.

cases.question: This figure has four scatter-plot panels labeled A, B, C, D. Which panel shows the strongest linear correlation? Briefly justify.
cases.intent: compare correlation strength across the four scatter panels labeled A-D and identify which is most linear
cases.what_it_tests: Comparative analysis across panels — the CharXiv-style dense scientific figure use case.

cases.question: Trace the shortest directed path from the node labeled 'start' to the node labeled 'end'. List the sequence of nodes visited.
cases.intent: identify all nodes by their labels, the directed edges connecting them, and the shortest path from start to end
cases.what_it_tests: Graph-structure extraction — the diagram-to-mermaid use case.

cases.question: What is the total amount paid on this receipt, and how many distinct items were ordered?
cases.intent: extract the exact final total in dollars and count the line items above the subtotal row
cases.what_it_tests: Document data extraction — the receipts/forms/tables use case.

cases.question: There's an error banner on this UI screenshot. What does the error say and what action does it suggest the user take?
cases.intent: find the error banner and quote the exact error message text and the suggested user action
cases.what_it_tests: UI screenshot triage — the debug-this-error-message use case.

cases.question: On this multi-series line chart, at timestamp t=42, what is the value of the 'memory_kb' series? Give me the number.
cases.intent: identify the line labeled 'memory_kb' in a 6-series line chart and read its y-value precisely at x=42
cases.what_it_tests: Precise per-series readout from a dense multi-line chart with a small legend — the kind of plot where Opus's tendency to summarize fails the question.

cases.question: Find the cell at the intersection of the row labeled exactly 'r5' and the column labeled exactly 'c7' (these are the literal tick-label strings printed on the axes, not 1-based positional indices). Use the viridis colorbar on the right to convert that cell's color to a numeric value.
cases.intent: find the single cell at the intersection of the row whose y-axis tick-label reads 'r5' (literal string) and the column whose x-axis tick-label reads 'c7' (literal string); read its color and convert via the viridis colorbar to a numeric value
cases.what_it_tests: Multi-step visual reasoning: locate-by-label → color → colorbar lookup → numeric value. Charts-with-legends class. Question is worded to defeat the row 5 (0-indexed) vs 5th row (1-indexed) ambiguity that earlier-version 0-indexed-position phrasing surfaced.

cases.question: There are four labeled clusters in this scatter plot, with arrows annotating each. Which cluster is centered at approximately (3, 5)? Give me its label.
cases.intent: identify all four labeled clusters in the scatter plot, their approximate centroid coordinates, and match the cluster whose centroid is closest to (3, 5)
cases.what_it_tests: Combining geometric reasoning (centroid position) with text-label binding (which annotation goes with which cluster).

cases.question: Return this flowchart as a JSON object with two arrays. nodes: each element has id (the node label as a string) and kind (one of start, decision, process, end). edges: each element has from (source id), to (target id), and label (the edge text, or empty string). Include every node and every edge visible.
cases.intent: extract a complete machine-readable representation of the flowchart: every node with its kind classification, every directed edge with its label, with no omissions
cases.what_it_tests: Structured-extraction task where the question explicitly asks for JSON. Tests both completeness (counting every node) and structural correctness (kind classification, edge labels).
